posts.author: rdvs34283
posts.title: America’s Michelangelo. Italian-born painter Constantino Brumidi emigrated to the United States in 1849 & became a naturalized citizen soon after. He honed his craft painting frescoes & murals in Rome, & left his imprint throughout the U.S Capitol, including the massive 4,664 sq ft dome fresco.

link_metadata.description: George Washington and Italian painter Constantino Brumidi are forever connected in a fresco adorning the dome of the U.S. Capitol.
link_metadata.title: 180 Feet Up: The Daring Story Behind the Capitol’s Most Famous Painting
